name: xschem-viewer-configurable
description: View XSchem .sch/.sym schematics in VS Code with configurable library search paths. Bundles the SKY130 and IHP SG13G2 device symbols so PDK schematics render with zero config, and can follow an xschemrc into an installed open PDK.

scripts.test: node test/resolver.test.cjs && node test/config.test.cjs && node test/manifest.test.cjs

xschem.libraryPaths.markdownDescription:
Additional XSchem library search roots (behaves like `XSCHEM_LIBRARY_PATH`). Each entry is a **directory**; a symbol reference such as `sg13g2_pr/sg13_lv_nmos.sym` is looked up as `<root>/sg13g2_pr/sg13_lv_nmos.sym`. Roots are searched in order, after the extension's bundled libraries and before the schematic-relative fallback. Entries that don't resolve to an existing directory are skipped (enable `#xschem.resolveDebug#` to log skips).

Variables: `${env:VAR}`; a leading `~`; `${workspaceFolder:NAME}` (a **named** folder from a multi-root workspace вАФ the unambiguous form); and bare `${workspaceFolder}` (the opened schematic's own workspace folder вАФ in a multi-root workspace this is the innermost/most-specific folder, e.g. a submodule root, **not** the meta-root). Absolute paths are used verbatim; relative paths resolve against the schematic's workspace folder.

Example (this workspace):
```json
"xschem.libraryPaths": [
  "${env:PDK_ROOT}/ihp-sg13g2/libs.tech/xschem",
  "${workspaceFolder:platform}/docker/xschem_library"
]
```
Or with absolute paths (never ambiguous):
```json
"xschem.libraryPaths": [
  "/home/you/pdks/ihp-sg13g2/libs.tech/xschem",
  "/abs/path/to/spicexplorer-platform/docker/xschem_library"
]
```
(In-repo `shared/вА¶` and sibling-block symbols are picked up automatically by `#xschem.autoDetectXschemrc#`.)

xschem.autoDetectXschemrc.markdownDescription: Walk up from the opened schematic's folder to the workspace root and add every directory that contains an `xschemrc` file as a library search root, **and** parse that file's `append XSCHEM_LIBRARY_PATH` lines (resolving the `[file dirname [info script]]` idiom). So in-repo `shared/вА¶` and sibling-block symbols resolve with no manual configuration. Parsed roots are only added if they exist and lie inside the workspace; lines using `$env(...)` or `source` are skipped вАФ PDK symbols outside the repo stay opt-in via an explicit `#xschem.libraryPaths#` entry.

xschem.followXschemrcPdkSource.markdownDescription:
**Opt-in.** When an in-repo `xschemrc` `source`s an **open-source PDK's** xschemrc вАФ e.g. `source $env(PDK_ROOT)/$env(PDK)/libs.tech/xschem/xschemrc` вАФ add that PDK's `libs.tech/xschem` directory as a library search root, so PDK devices resolve without a manual `#xschem.libraryPaths#` entry.

Requires `PDK_ROOT` to be set in the environment, and the PDK name to come either from an inline `set ::env(PDK) вА¶` in the `xschemrc` or a `PDK` environment variable. Only **open** PDKs are followed: the resolved `вА¶/<pdk>/libs.tech/xschem` path segment must match `sky130*`, `gf180mcu*`, `ihp-sg13g2`, or `sg13g2` вАФ any other (proprietary/foundry) PDK is refused, as is a path with an unresolved variable.

This deliberately reaches **outside** the workspace, which is why it is off by default; leave it off and list the path in `#xschem.libraryPaths#` if you prefer to be explicit. Independent of `#xschem.autoDetectXschemrc#`, which only ever adds in-workspace directories.

xschem.includeWorkspaceFolders.markdownDescription: When enabled, allow the viewer to load symbol/schematic files from the schematic's **own** workspace folder (enabling relative `../` sub-block references anywhere within that folder). Off by default to keep the webview's file-read scope minimal вАФ the schematic's directory plus the configured `#xschem.libraryPaths#` (and auto-detected `xschemrc` roots) are always available regardless of this setting. Only the schematic's own workspace folder is exposed, never sibling roots of a multi-root workspace.

xschem.resolveDebug.description: Log every symbol-resolution attempt to the webview Developer Tools console (Help > Toggle Developer Tools). Use this to debug "symbol not found" problems and see exactly which paths were tried.

author: NooriDan
